entries.title: Industrial Revolution
entries.content: Period from roughly 1760 to 1840 when manufacturing shifted from hand tools to machines, steam power, and factories. It began in Britain and transformed transport, textiles, and urban life.

entries.title: World War II Overview
entries.content: Global war (1939–1945) between Axis powers (Germany, Italy, Japan) and Allies (UK, USSR, USA, France, China). Ended with Allied victory; led to United Nations and Cold War.

entries.title: Ancient Egypt
entries.content: Civilization along the Nile (~3100 BCE–30 BCE). Known for pharaohs, pyramids, hieroglyphic writing, and advanced agriculture using annual Nile floods.

entries.title: French Revolution
entries.content: 1789–1799 uprising against monarchy and feudalism in France. Key ideas: liberty, equality, fraternity. Led to Napoleon Bonaparte's rise.

entries.title: Cold War
entries.content: Geopolitical tension (1947–1991) between USA and USSR without direct large-scale fighting. Featured nuclear arms race, space race, and proxy wars.

entries.title: Renaissance
entries.content: Cultural rebirth in Europe (14th–17th century). Revived classical learning, art, and science. Figures: Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, Galileo.
